page: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the DLDSv5 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, configured with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The memory configuration provides 96.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled with a 1843 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Benchmark results place the D48lds_v5 in the average tier for most general compute tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 46589.55 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15804.0. However, it demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, achieving 218.81 GB/sec in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and a PassMark CPU encryption test score of 41963.43, both ranking in the top 25% of tested systems. For language model tasks, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 269.44 tokens/sec with a small 135M model, though performance drops to a weak 2.75 tokens/sec for large 70B models.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D48lds_v5 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications. However, this lower memory allocation relative to the 48 dedicated vCPUs offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. The inclusion of a 1843 GB local disk provides high-capacity local storage without requiring external attachments, balancing the compute-to-storage ratio.

This server configuration is suited for multi-threaded CPU-bound tasks, cryptographic operations, image processing, and software compilation. The combination of 48 vCPUs and local storage also supports database operations and web serving workloads that fit within the 96.0 GB memory boundary.
description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v5 is an x86_64 server from the DLDSv5 family, powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs. It features 96.0 GB of RAM, establishing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 1843 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (218.81 GB/sec) and CPU encryption tests, while maintaining average scores in general Geekbench and stress-ng benchmarks. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive tasks that do not require large memory footprints. This server is suited for software compilation, cryptographic processing, image processing, and database operations constrained within its memory limits.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v5 is a Hyper-V virtualized server featuring 48 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, optimized for compute-heavy and storage-integrated workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v5 features 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, optimized for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-focused server featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs, low memory density, and integrated high-capacity local storage for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
Integrated 1843 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong performance in multi-threaded decompression and cryptographic encryption
Suited for software compilation, image processing, and database operations
